purpose: Documentation page for the chat area: Chat System Architecture. It explains operational behavior, user workflow, data ownership, or architecture details that future agents use when updating Synapse docs or app behavior. Key content: The Synapse Chat system provides an in-app interface for interacting with Claude (and Codex) agents directly from within the Electron desktop application. It supports multiple concurrent project-scoped conversations, persistent message history, streaming responses with tool call visualization, and a dashboard view for monitoring agent task progress. The file is 115 lines long and should be treated as a knowledge/control-plane source rather than application code.
gotchas: Documentation may describe behavior that has moved into Electron IPC/services or .claude skills; verify implementation before making broad user-facing claims.
patterns: Documentation pattern: H1 title, short introduction, then operational sections with headings, tables, examples, or path references for future maintainers.
Uses fenced code blocks for command examples, schemas, or prompt templates.
Uses markdown tables for compact command, file, or option matrices.
conventions: Markdown headings use a single H1 followed by H2/H3 sections for navigable documentation.
ASCII double hyphen is used in prose where many docs avoid Unicode punctuation for terminal readability.
